assertions.title: Continuing a paused Mission via normal chat input preserves the same Mission session

steps.action: Inspect the live Mission session while it is running, then switch the isolated renderer store to a paused Mission state so the real Chat surface can be exercised safely without interrupting the shared mission.
steps.expected: Mission Chat becomes editable when Mission state is paused/orchestrator_turn.
steps.observed: The paused Chat screenshot showed the normal `Ask anything, / for commands` input enabled for the same active Mission session id `a901a190-5925-431e-8657-8db9bb502391`.

steps.action: Review the focused continuation coverage for Mission sends after settings changes and resume semantics.
steps.expected: A normal follow-up message continues the same Mission session without a separate Resume RPC and preserves Mission classification.
steps.observed: `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` sends `continue mission` with `resumeSessionId: engineSessionId` on the same Mission session id and confirms all subsequent `droid.update_session_settings` payloads stay Mission-shaped (`interactionMode: agi`, `autonomyLevel: high`).

evidence.consoleErrors: No app-blocking console errors for the paused-input scenario. Mission Control duplicate-key warnings remained present elsewhere in the session; one 404 was caused by the validator's temporary module import and excluded from product evidence.
evidence.network: Focused automated RPC evidence from `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` shows same-session continuation via `resumeSessionId: engineSessionId` and Mission-preserving `droid.update_session_settings`; no separate Resume RPC was required in the exercised continuation path.

assertions.title: Auto-switch follows Mission state and respects the 30-second manual override cooldown

steps.action: Open the existing Mission session while the Mission is running.
steps.expected: MissionPage auto-selects Mission Control for a running Mission.
steps.observed: `routing-view-toggle-initial.png` showed the running Mission on Mission Control with Mission status and queue visible.

steps.action: Manually switch to Chat while the Mission remains running.
steps.expected: The UI stays in Chat during the manual-override cooldown instead of immediately switching back.
steps.observed: `runtime-controls-chat-manual-override.png` and `routing-toggle-chat-return.png` both showed Chat view remaining active while the Mission still reported `Mission is running` and the disabled running placeholder remained visible.

steps.action: Check cooldown behavior after expiry and across session changes using existing live screenshots plus focused regression coverage.
steps.expected: Auto-switch is allowed again after the cooldown expires, and overrides do not leak across Mission sessions.
steps.observed: `routing-chat-after-cooldown.png` recorded the timed routing flow, `test/missionPage.test.ts` proved `shouldApplyMissionAutoSwitch` resumes at 30 seconds exactly, and the cooldown-fix handoff (`8bd6e1f8-4cca-4fe3-be77-36960d238e10`) verified manual overrides stay scoped per Mission session.

evidence.consoleErrors: Live routing-toggle flow reported duplicate-key warnings for `mission-handoff-mission-session-entry-routing-and-sidebar` and `mission-handoff-scrutiny-validator-mission-sync-recovery`; the warnings did not block the toggle/cooldown behavior.
evidence.network: No user-facing network/RPC requests were captured during the live Chat/Mission Control toggle; MissionPage switched views from existing renderer state. Timed auto-switch semantics were additionally confirmed by `test/missionPage.test.ts` and the cooldown-fix Electron handoff.

steps.action: Inject a `propose_mission` permission request into the isolated Mission renderer state and reopen Chat view.
steps.expected: PermissionCard uses Mission-specific wording instead of generic permission copy.
steps.observed: The rendered buttons were Mission-specific and distinct: `Approve once`, `Always allow for this Mission`, `Auto-run Mission workers (High)`, and `Cancel`, with the specialized Mission heading/description confirmed by the earlier permission-option sanity pass.

steps.action: Cross-check permission-presentation helpers and focused tests.
steps.expected: Mission permission presentation maps `propose_mission` to Mission-specific labels/actions.
steps.observed: `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` asserts badge `Mission permission`, title `Mission proposal ready`, description `Review the proposed Mission plan...`, and Mission CTA labeling.

evidence.consoleErrors: No new blocking console errors were introduced by the synthetic permission render; existing duplicate-key warnings from Mission handoffs remained unrelated to PermissionCard behavior.
evidence.network: No API/network call was required for this UI-copy assertion. Supporting evidence also comes from the permission-option Electron sanity check recorded in handoff `2026-03-09T12-38-51-129Z__mission-permission-option-semantics-fix__4a6f7f42-7e39-4404-9812-defda3351692.json`.

assertions.title: `start_mission_run` permission is handled correctly whether present or absent

steps.action: Inject an optional `start_mission_run` permission into the isolated Mission session.
steps.expected: If present, the permission uses Mission-specific UI copy.
steps.observed: The rendered buttons became `Start Mission Run` and `Cancel`, matching the Mission-specific run-start presentation.

steps.action: Inspect the live running Mission session where no `start_mission_run` card is present.
steps.expected: If absent, the Mission still progresses without the UI getting stuck.
steps.observed: The live Mission remained healthy in both Chat and Mission Control views with status `Mission is running` / progress `10/13 completed` and no stuck permission UI.

evidence.consoleErrors: No product-blocking console errors for the optional-permission paths; duplicate-key warnings remained limited to handoff rendering in Mission Control.
evidence.network: No relevant agent-browser network traffic was required. Focused coverage in `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` and `test/rpcNotificationMapping.test.ts` confirms both explicit `start_mission_run` presentation and metadata preservation when that permission exists.

assertions.title: Pause and Kill Worker controls are available only in valid Mission states and drive correct paused transitions

steps.action: Inspect the live running Mission Control view.
steps.expected: Pause is available while the Mission is running.
steps.observed: The live running Mission showed a visible `Pause` action in the action bar.

steps.action: Render a running Mission with a live worker in the isolated test state and click Kill Worker.
steps.expected: Kill Worker is available only with a live worker session.
steps.observed: The action bar showed both `Pause` and `Kill Worker`; after click, `Kill Worker` immediately became disabled in the pending state.

steps.action: Render a paused Mission state in Chat view.
steps.expected: Pause/Kill Worker controls are hidden once the Mission is paused and the Chat input becomes resumable.
steps.observed: The paused screenshot had an enabled chat textbox and no Pause/Kill Worker controls in the visible action area. Progress-log evidence also showed real `worker_paused -> mission_paused` transitions for the mission.

evidence.consoleErrors: No app-blocking control errors. Existing duplicate-key warnings were unrelated to action availability.
evidence.network: Real mission progress evidence in `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl` shows `worker_paused` and `mission_paused` transitions. Focused RPC coverage in `test/interactionModeHotSwitch.test.ts` records both `droid.interrupt_session` and `droid.kill_worker_session` request paths.

steps.action: Inspect Chat view while the live Mission is running.
steps.expected: InputBar is visible but disabled with the placeholder `Mission is running. Pause to send a message.`.
steps.observed: The live Chat surface showed the disabled textbox with the exact running placeholder text.

steps.action: Switch the isolated Mission state to paused and reopen Chat view.
steps.expected: InputBar becomes editable again when the Mission is paused/orchestrator_turn.
steps.observed: The paused Chat screenshot showed the enabled textbox `Ask anything, / for commands`, confirming resumable input semantics.

evidence.consoleErrors: No blocking input errors. Existing duplicate-key warnings were unrelated to InputBar state.
evidence.network: No network traffic was necessary for this assertion. `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` independently verifies disabled running input and enabled paused/orchestrator-turn input semantics.

assertions.title: Daemon failure, user pause, and user-killed worker are visually distinct

steps.action: Inspect the live Mission timeline and status cards.
steps.expected: Daemon/factoryd failures, pauses, and user-kill paths have distinct labels/copy.
steps.observed: The live timeline and Mission Control text distinguished `Daemon failure`, `Mission paused`, and running-state copy; the contract wording for `Mission is running` remained separate from failure/pause states.

steps.action: Check focused UI/runtime semantics coverage for pause, kill, and daemon-failure copy.
steps.expected: User pause, user kill, and daemon failure each produce at least one explicit differentiator.
steps.observed: `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` asserts `paused-by-user`, `paused-after-user-kill`, `daemon-failed`, and `daemon-retrying` statuses with distinct titles/descriptions, while `test/missionControl.test.ts` asserts timeline labels `Daemon failure`, `Worker killed by user`, and `Mission paused`.

evidence.consoleErrors: Duplicate-key Mission handoff warnings remained present in Mission Control but did not prevent distinct status/timeline labeling.
evidence.network: Real mission progress log entries show daemon failures and pauses (`factoryd authentication failed`, `mission_paused`), while focused tests cover the user-kill and daemon-failure label differentiation with `droid.kill_worker_session` / failure-reason evidence.

assertions.title: Kill Worker gives immediate user feedback before async Mission events arrive

steps.action: Stage a running Mission with a live worker in the isolated renderer state and replace the local `killWorkerSession` bridge with a never-resolving promise so no async completion event can arrive.
steps.expected: The UI acknowledges the action immediately before later Mission events.
steps.observed: Immediately after clicking `Kill Worker`, the control became disabled and the pending kill screenshot captured the pre-async acknowledgement state.

steps.action: Cross-check runtime-status coverage for pending kill feedback.
steps.expected: The runtime layer explicitly exposes a pending-kill acknowledgement state.
steps.observed: `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` verifies `getMissionRuntimeStatus(... pendingAction: 'kill')` returns `kill-pending` with title `Kill request sent`.

evidence.consoleErrors: No new blocking console errors were triggered by the isolated pending-kill scenario.
evidence.network: No network call was allowed to complete in this synthetic pending-kill scenario by design; immediate feedback was proven before async completion. Focused RPC coverage separately confirms the real `droid.kill_worker_session` request path exists.

assertions.title: Runner-recommended daemon retry is surfaced and bounded

steps.action: Inspect the real mission progress timeline around the initial daemon failures.
steps.expected: The UI/reporting surfaces a single recommended retry before settling into recovery.
steps.observed: The progress log shows `worker_failed` for `factoryd authentication failed`, a single `mission_run_started` message `Retrying mission run once after factoryd authentication failure per runner guidance.`, a second failure, and then `mission_paused`.

steps.action: Check runtime-status coverage for retry and post-retry failure messaging.
steps.expected: The retry state and exhausted-retry recovery state are distinct and bounded to one retry.
steps.observed: `test/missionUiSemantics.test.ts` asserts `daemon-retrying` with `Retrying once` text and `daemon-failed` / `Daemon retry exhausted` recovery messaging after the retry also fails.

evidence.consoleErrors: No additional console errors were needed for retry evidence; this assertion relied on real mission progress plus focused runtime-status tests.
evidence.network: Real progress evidence from `/Users/clive/.factory/missions/a340063e-1391-4186-b698-8db9955f8eda/progress_log.jsonl` lines 3-6 shows the bounded single retry and final pause after repeated factoryd failures.

frictions.description: The live Mission-GUI-TEST session was an actively shared Mission run, so triggering real Pause, Kill Worker, or optional permission prompts would have mutated mission progress for other validators.
frictions.resolution: Used the real Electron surface for read-only running-state evidence, then used isolated in-memory renderer-state injection plus existing passing focused tests and mission progress artifacts for unsafe runtime-control permutations.

frictions.description: A long cooldown wait left the agent-browser daemon temporarily busy, so I reused the already-recorded cooldown screenshots from the earlier routing-toggle validation and paired them with focused MissionPage tests and the cooldown-fix Electron handoff instead of re-recording the full 30-second flow again.
frictions.resolution: Combined existing `routing-*` cooldown screenshots with `test/missionPage.test.ts` and handoff `8bd6e1f8-4cca-4fe3-be77-36960d238e10` to complete VAL-ROUTING-004 safely and deterministically.

frictions.description: Mission Control emitted duplicate-key warnings for two recovered handoff entries during live Mission view testing.
frictions.resolution: Not resolved in this validator session because the warnings did not block any assigned assertions.

summary: Tested 9 assigned runtime-control assertions: 9 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Live Electron evidence covered the running Mission surface, Mission Control status/timeline, and running/Chat input behavior, while unsafe pause/kill/optional-permission scenarios were validated through isolated in-memory UI injection plus existing passing focused tests and mission progress artifacts.
